zone_6b_notes: Direct sow after last frost — May 15 in Farmington, timing for Halloween harvest (count back 90–120 days). Missouri growing season fits most standard pumpkin varieties. Needs plenty of space — standard varieties spread 10–15 feet; plant at garden edge or in dedicated patch. Harvest when fully colored, stem is dry and corky, and skin resists scratching. Squash vine borers are the primary threat in Missouri — monitor for orange eggs on stem near base, remove by hand early. Cure at room temperature for 10 days; stores 2–3 months.

planting.best_planting_time: Direct sow mid-May; start indoors late April if desired

water: 1–1.5 inches/week; avoid overhead watering — wet foliage causes powdery mildew
